Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 
2nd May 2026  

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East 
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief.
  
● Israeli forces struck the southern Lebanese village of Zawtar with white phosphorus shells.

● UAE announces the full resumption of normal air navigation operations across its airspace.

● India-linked LPG tanker, SARV SHAKTI, was making a rare transit through Strait of Hormuz with 45,000 tons of LPG. Its full transit could take ~10-14 hours.

● President Trump announced he is raising tariffs on cars and trucks from the EU to 25%, effective next week.

● China's Commerce Ministry today issued an injunction blocking U.S. sanctions on five Chinese refineries buying Iranian oil, shielding its energy imports.

● Seven OPEC+ member countries have reached a preliminary agreement to increase oil production targets by roughly 188,000 barrels per day in June, despite UAE’s exit.

● The Trump administration announced over $8.6 billion in emergency arms sales to Middle Eastern partners today, including Israel, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE.

END OF THE BRIEF

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
1st May 2026

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East

According to a report cited by The Wall Street Journal, Iran is considering unconventional tactics, including the possible use of trained dolphins to carry mines targeting U.S. naval vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

The concept is not entirely new; Iran reportedly acquired trained marine mammals from the Soviet Union in 2000, with capabilities linked to explosives delivery and underwater missions.

Comparing the Strait of Hormuz to Great Nicobar based solely on physical width is a 19th-century view of naval warfare. With BrahMos missiles (range 450km–800km) and P-8I Poseidon aircraft operating from the islands, India can monitor and interdict the entire 200km Six Degree Channel with 100% overlap. Physical narrowness is irrelevant when the entire gap is within high-precision strike range.
